steps.title: Identify the right triangle and angle position
steps.content: Triangle JKL has a right angle at K (shown by the square symbol). The hypotenuse JL = 59 connects vertices J and L. Angle $y°$ is at vertex L. We need to find the length of side $\overline{KL}$, which is adjacent to angle $y°$.

steps.title: Apply the cosine ratio
steps.content:
In a right triangle, $\cos(\text{angle}) = \frac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$.

From angle $y°$ at vertex L, the adjacent side is $KL$ and the hypotenuse is $JL = 59$.

Therefore: $$\cos y° = \frac{KL}{59}$$ We are given $\cos y° = \frac{58}{59}$, so: $$\frac{KL}{59} = \frac{58}{59}$$ Multiplying both sides by 59 gives $KL = 58$.

The answer is 58.
